Name: Caterpie
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antennae on its head to drive away enemies.

Name: Metapod
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.
Flavor-Text: A steel-hard shell protects its tender body. It quietly endures hardships while awaiting evolution.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward. If you attached Energy in this way, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It loves the honey of flowers and can locate flower patches that have even tiny amounts of pollen.

Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn’t bother it.

Name: Seedot
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.

Name: Nuzleaf
Flavor-Text: The sound of its grass flute makes its listeners uneasy. It lives deep in forests.

Name: Shiftry
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [G] Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe’s mind and take preemptive action.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It uses the different poisons in each hand separately when it attacks. The stronger its aroma, the healthier it is.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Luring prey with a sweet scent, it uses poison whips on its arms to poison, bind, and finish off the prey.

Name: Maractus
Effects: During your next turn, flip 6 coins instead of 2 for this Pokémon’s Prickly Needles attack.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

Name: Charizard-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard-EX
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Combustion Blast during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Charizard-EX
Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: You find abandoned coal mines full of them. They dig tirelessly in search of coal.

Name: Fletchinder
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

Name: Pyroar
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Basic Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

Name: Qwilfish
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It eats anything, so it can even live in polluted streams and lakes. No one pays any attention to it.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack) you may Knock Out this Pokémon. If you do, attach 3 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-EX).
Flavor-Text: Milotic is breathtakingly beautiful. Those that see it are said to forget their combative spirits.

Name: Spheal
Flavor-Text: They can’t swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they’re happy they clap fins.

Name: Sealeo
Effects: Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It has a very sensitive nose. It touches new things with its nose to examine them.

Name: Walrein
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
This attack does 120 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t draw a card at the beginning of his or her next turn.
Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It lives in warm seas. It is said that a couple finding this Pokémon will be blessed with eternal love.

Name: Buizel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It swims by rotating its two tails like a screw. When it dives, its flotation sac collapses.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: Shuffle 3 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

Name: Bergmite
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It blocks opponents’ attacks with the ice that shields its body. It uses cold air to repair any cracks with new ice.

Name: Avalugg
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard any Stadium card in play.
Flavor-Text: Its ice-covered body is as hard as steel. Its cumbersome frame crushes anything that stands in its way.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Effects: This attack does 40 damage times the number of Prize cards your opponent has taken.
Flavor-Text: Its claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.

Name: Luxray
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Discard a Trainer card you find there.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When its eyes gleam gold, it can spot hiding prey–even those taking shelter behind a wall.

Name: Magnezone-EX
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent’s discard pile onto his or her Bench.
Put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

Name: Dusclops
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Anyone who dares peer into its body to see its spectral ball of fire will have their spirit stolen away.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: Put damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon equal to the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.

Name: Toxicroak-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Espurr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out.

Name: Meowstic
Effects: Move as many damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent’s other Pokémon in any way you like.
Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust.

Name: Skrelp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Camouflaged as rotten kelp, they spray liquid poison on prey that approaches unawares and then finish it off.

Name: Geodude
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Found in fields and mountains. Mistaking them for boulders, people often step or trip on them.

Name: Graveler
Effects: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: With a free and uncaring nature, it doesn’t mind if pieces break off while it rolls down mountains.

Name: Golem
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack 30 more damage.
This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It tumbles down mountains, leaving grooves from peak to base. Stay clear of these grooves.

Name: Binacle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

Name: Barbaracle
Effects: Discard as many [F] Energy cards as you like from your hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
Flavor-Text: When they evolve, two Binacle multiply into seven. They fight with the power of seven Binacle.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on eggs stolen from nests. Its sharply hooked claws rip vulnerable spots on prey.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on eggs stolen from nests. Its sharply hooked claws rip vulnerable spots on prey.

Name: Weavile
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in snowy regions. It carves patterns in trees with its claws as a signal to others.

Name: Stunky
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It sprays a foul fluid from its rear. Its stench spreads over a mile radius, driving Pokémon away.

Name: Stunky
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It sprays a foul fluid from its rear. Its stench spreads over a mile radius, driving Pokémon away.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It sprays a vile-smelling fluid from the tip of its tail to attack. Its range is over 160 feet.

Name: Sandile
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It moves along below the sand’s surface, except for its nose and eyes. A dark membrane shields its eyes from the sun.

Name: Krokorok
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

Name: Scraggy
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling it skin up to its neck.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is.

Name: Forretress
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It is encased in a steel shell. Its peering eyes are all that can be seen of its mysterious innards.

Name: Durant
Effects: Discard cards from your opponent’s hand at random until he or she has 4 cards in his or her hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Individuals each play different roles in driving Heatmor, their natural predator, away from their colony.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It draws out and controls the hidden power of flowers. The flower Flabébé holds is most likely part of its body.

Name: Flabébé
Flavor-Text: It draws out and controls the hidden power of flowers. The flower Flabébé holds is most likely part of its body.

Name: Floette
Ability-Effect: Each of your [G] Pokémon in play gets +20 HP.
Flavor-Text: It flutters around fields of flowers and cares for flowers that are starting to wilt. It draws out the hidden power of flowers to battle.

Name: Floette
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It flutters around fields of flowers and cares for flowers that are starting to wilt. It draws out the hidden power of flowers to battle.

Name: Florges
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It claims exquisite flower gardens as its territory, and it obtains power from basking in the energy emitted by flowering plants.

Name: Spritzee
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It emits a scent that enraptures those who smell it. This fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten.

Name: Carbink
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [Y] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Born from temperatures and pressures deep underground, it fires beams from the stone in its head.

Name: M Charizard-EX
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

Name: Dragalge
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon can’t retreat.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.

Name: Goomy
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn’t dry out.

Name: Sliggoo
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything.

Name: Goodra
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

Name: Pidgey
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

Name: Pidgeotto
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Strong Gust attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

Name: Kangaskhan-EX
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Kangaskhan-EX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Asleep, flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Name: Sentret
Flavor-Text: When acting as a lookout, it warns others of danger by screeching and hitting the ground with its tail.

Name: Furret
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The mother puts its offspring to sleep by curling up around them. It corners foes with speed.

Name: Miltank
Effects: If you have any Stage 2 Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If it is around babies, the milk it produces contains much more nutrition than usual.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.

Name: Lopunny
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Extremely cautious, it quickly bounds off when it senses danger.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

Name: Furfrou
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

Name: Blacksmith
Description: Attach 2 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your [R] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fiery Torch
Description: Discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. (If you can’t discard a [R] Energy card, you can’t play this card.) Draw 2 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lysandre
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Magnetic Storm
Description: Each Pokémon in play has no Resistance.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Pal Pad
Description: Shuffle 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Center Lady
Description: Heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Fan Club
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Protection Cube
Description: Prevent all damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks it uses.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Sacred Ash
Description: Shuffle 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Startling Megaphone
Description: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Trick Shovel
Description: Look at the top card of either player’s deck. You may discard that card or return it to the top of the deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Charizard-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magnezone-EX
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Toxicroak-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kangaskhan-EX
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lysandre
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Center Lady
Description: Heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Fan Club
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: M Charizard-EX
Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Charizard-EX
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Kangaskhan-EX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blacksmith
Description: Attach 2 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your [R] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).
